description: Formal visual semantics registry for LENS NextGen executive rendering. Defines semantic rules for readiness badges, qualifier chips, signal severity, pressure tiers, propagation roles, blocked/diagnostic states, evidence visibility, audit visibility, and topology display.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Readiness Badge State Token Mapping
visual_semantics_rules.description: Readiness badge visual treatment is determined solely by the ReadinessState value from the report_object. No aesthetic override is permitted. Design token is derived from governance state.
forbidden: raw ReadinessState enum values in badge text

visual_semantics_rules.name: Readiness Badge Visual Priority Enforcement
visual_semantics_rules.description: Readiness badge is the highest-priority visual element. It must be the first rendered element in REGION_EXECUTIVE_HEADER. Cannot be minimized, hidden, or visually de-emphasized below any other element.

requirements.minimum_visual_scale: proportional — never smaller than supporting elements

visual_semantics_rules.name: Qualifier Chip Token and Label Mapping
visual_semantics_rules.description: Qualifier chip visual treatment is determined by Q-taxonomy class. Chip label is from vocabulary contract. Raw Q-xx values forbidden in display.

Q-04.absence_notice: Signal intelligence withheld from this view.

forbidden: raw Q-00..Q-04 text in chip label

visual_semantics_rules.name: Qualifier Chip Persistence Across Viewport Sizes
visual_semantics_rules.description: Qualifier chip must remain visible at all viewport sizes. It cannot be hidden to simplify the header on narrow screens. It may reflow to a second line in the header but must not disappear.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Propagation Role Visual Encoding
visual_semantics_rules.description: Propagation role is visually encoded with a distinct indicator per role. Role labels use vocabulary contract approved terms. Raw enum values forbidden.
forbidden: raw PropagationRole enum values (ORIGIN, RECEIVER, PASS_THROUGH, ISOLATED) in visual display

visual_semantics_rules.name: Propagation Path Visual Rendering Rules
visual_semantics_rules.description: Propagation path is rendered as an ordered domain alias sequence. ALI-04 aliases must be used. No raw cluster keys. No animated flow. Direction is structural, not temporal.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Pressure Tier Visual Token Mapping
visual_semantics_rules.description: Pressure tier visual treatment uses design tokens derived from PressureTier enum. Numerical values are forbidden in all pressure indicators. Token colors are governance-derived, not aesthetic.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Signal Card Pressure Indicator Rendering
visual_semantics_rules.description: Signal cards render pressure tier as a labeled indicator using the pressure token. No numerical signal values. No threshold bar charts. No comparison indicators without grounded comparative evidence.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Diagnostic State Visual Rendering
visual_semantics_rules.description: Diagnostic state renders an explicit banner in REGION_DIAGNOSTIC and a diagnostic border on affected panels. Content remains visible. The word 'advisory' must appear in the diagnostic notice.

region_diagnostic_banner.text: This report contains content under advisory review. Advisory confirmation recommended.

rendering.content_visibility: VISIBLE — content shown with diagnostic frame

forbidden: blank panel instead of diagnostic notice

visual_semantics_rules.name: Q-03 Diagnostic Frame Application
visual_semantics_rules.description: When qualifier_class = Q-03, a diagnostic frame is applied to WHY and READINESS_STATE panels. Advisory notice is added: 'Advisory confirmation recommended before executive action.'

rendering.advisory_notice_text: Advisory confirmation recommended before executive action.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Blocked State Full Module Rendering
visual_semantics_rules.description: Blocked state renders an explicit blocked notice replacing intelligence content. All panels enter BLOCKED state. Blocked notice uses token-blocked visual treatment.

rendering.content_replacement: FULL — blocked notice replaces all intelligence content

forbidden: silent degradation to lower readiness state
partial intelligence rendering from failed evidence

visual_semantics_rules.name: No Silent Blocked Degradation
visual_semantics_rules.description: Blocked state must never silently degrade. No blocked module may render without its explicit notice. The blocked token is visually distinct from all readiness tokens.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Evidence Drawer Visual Access Enforcement
visual_semantics_rules.description: Evidence drawers must always be accessible within one interaction from the domain evidence summary. Evidence access cannot disappear based on viewport, audience tier (for EXECUTIVE+), or rendering state.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Evidence Content Visual Immutability
visual_semantics_rules.description: Evidence content in drawers must be visually identical to committed evidence_blocks[] content. No editorial visual overlay. No 'simplified view' toggle that removes evidence detail.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Topology Display Read-Only Enforcement
visual_semantics_rules.description: Topology region is read-only. No user interaction may modify topology display state as a persistent change. Navigation interactions (zoom, select domain) are display-only.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Topology Simplification for Executive Readability
visual_semantics_rules.description: Topology display must be simplified to executive readability. Domain-level by default. Cluster detail on expand. No numerical topology data. Pressure indicators use tokens, not numbers.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Audit Region Audience-Gating Enforcement
visual_semantics_rules.description: REGION_AUDIT and full lineage references are audience-gated to AUDIT tier. Executive and Advisory tiers do not see REGION_AUDIT by default. No client-side tier elevation.

visual_semantics_rules.name: Audit Reference Visual Distinction
visual_semantics_rules.description: Audit reference elements (hash values, stream anchors, run IDs) use a visually distinct typographic treatment (monospace; muted) to distinguish them from intelligence content.
forbidden: presenting hash values as primary content typography
adding explanatory text that interprets hash value meaning

design_token_governance.description: Design tokens are governance outputs, not aesthetic preferences. Token values derive from governance state mappings.
